early.description: Small, pale to dark green, irregular spots on leaves.

mid.description: Spots expand rapidly, turning brown/black with white fuzz on undersides.

late.description: Whole leaves and stems blighted; severe plant collapse.

solution.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Metalaxyl 8% + Mancozeb 64% WP @ 2.5g/L
solution.application: Foliar spray immediately upon symptom observation, repeat after 7-10 days.
solution.organic_alternative: Copper-based fungicides or Trichoderma viride formulation.

early.description: Small brown/black spots with concentric rings.

mid.description: Spots merge, lower leaves start yellowing and dropping.

late.description: Extensive defoliation affecting fruit size and quality.

solution.pesticide: Chlorothalonil 75% WP @ 2g/L or Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L
solution.application: Spray at 10-day intervals upon first sign of disease.
solution.organic_alternative: Bacillus subtilis spray.

agro_zones: East Coast Plains and Hills Region

early.description: Spindle-shaped spots with gray centers on leaves.

mid.description: Spots enlarge and coalesce, killing entire leaves.

late.description: Neck blast phase leading to completely empty/white panicles.

solution.pesticide: Tricyclazole 75% WP @ 0.6g/L or Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L
solution.application: Spray at tillering and panicle emergence stages.
solution.organic_alternative: Pseudomonas fluorescens @ 10g/L.

early.description: Oval to cylindrical brown spots on leaves.

mid.description: Numerous spots causing leaves to dry out.

late.description: Severe spotting on grains, reducing quality and weight.

solution.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2g/L or Carbendazim 50% WP @ 1g/L
solution.application: Spray during tillering and heading stages.
solution.organic_alternative: Application of adequate Nitrogen and Potassium fertilizers.

agro_zones: West Coast Plains and Ghats Region

mid.description: Streaks turn yellow to white, leaves dry and curl.

late.description: Kresek (seedling wilt) or severe drying of mature plants.

solution.pesticide: Streptomycin sulphate + Tetracycline combination @ 0.3g/L + Copper Oxychloride @ 2.5g/L
solution.application: Spray at initial disease appearance. Avoid excess nitrogen.
solution.organic_alternative: Cow dung extract spray (20%).

early.description: Yellow pustules forming parallel lines on leaves.

mid.description: Pustules cover most of the leaf area, plant vigour drops.

late.description: Severe shriveling of grains, massive yield reduction.

solution.pesticide: Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L or Tebuconazole 25.9% EC @ 1ml/L
solution.application: Spray when disease is seen, repeat after 15 days if needed.
solution.organic_alternative: Use resistant varieties (e.g., HD 2967).

early.description: Scattered brown pustules on upper leaf surface.

mid.description: Increased pustule density causing leaf drying.

late.description: Early leaf death resulting in reduced grain weight.

solution.pesticide: Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L or Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L
solution.application: Foliar spray at disease onset.
solution.organic_alternative: Resistant varieties and balanced fertilization.

early.description: Yellowing/drying of top leaves; red lesions on midribs.

mid.description: Canes shrink, inner tissues turn red with white cross-bands; sour smell.

late.description: Complete clump drying and total loss of sugar recovery.

solution.pesticide: Thiophanate Methyl 70% WP @ 1g/L for sett treatment
solution.application: Treat setts before planting. Uproot and burn affected clumps.
solution.organic_alternative: Trichoderma viride sett treatment and soil application.

early.description: Upward curling of leaves and thickening of veins.

mid.description: Formation of leaf-like enations on veins; stunted growth.

solution.pesticide: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 0.5ml/L or Thiamethoxam 25% WG @ 0.4g/L (to control Whitefly vector)
solution.application: Spray vector control insecticides. Uproot infected plants.
solution.organic_alternative: Neem seed kernel extract (NSKE) 5%.

early.description: Yellow/white stripes at the base of leaves.

mid.description: Downy fungal growth on lower leaf surfaces; malformed tassels.

late.description: Crazy top symptom, no cob formation.

solution.pesticide: Metalaxyl MZ 72 WP @ 2.5g/L
solution.application: Foliar spray at 15-20 days after sowing.
solution.organic_alternative: Seed treatment with Trichoderma.

early.description: Cigar-shaped grayish-green to tan spots on lower leaves.

mid.description: Lesions expand and coalesce; upper leaves get infected.

late.description: Complete blighting of leaves reducing grain fill.

solution.pesticide: Mancozeb 75% WP @ 2.5g/L or Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L
solution.application: Spray upon noticing symptoms.
solution.organic_alternative: Crop rotation and burying residues.

agro_zones: West Coast Plains and Ghats Region

early.description: Light and dark green mottling/mosaic pattern on leaves.

mid.description: Leaf distortion, fern-like appearance, stunted plant.

late.description: Severely reduced fruit yield and internal fruit browning.

solution.pesticide: None (Viral disease)
solution.application: Remove and destroy infected plants. Wash hands and tools.
solution.organic_alternative: Sanitation, resistant varieties, and controlling vector insects.

agro_zones: East Coast Plains and Hills Region

early.description: Small, circular, sunken spots on ripe fruits.

mid.description: Spots expand with black concentric rings of fungal structures.

late.description: Fruits rot completely, branch die-back occurs.

solution.pesticide: Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L or Carbendazim 50% WP @ 1g/L
solution.application: Spray when fruits start ripening.
solution.organic_alternative: Pseudomonas fluorescens spray.

early.description: Small tan to dark brown lesions on lower leaves.

mid.description: Pustules erupt releasing spores; leaves turn yellow and drop.

late.description: Premature defoliation resulting in few, small pods.

solution.pesticide: Hexaconazole 5% EC @ 1ml/L or Propiconazole 25% EC @ 1ml/L
solution.application: Apply at the first sign of disease and repeat after 15 days.
solution.organic_alternative: Early maturing varieties.

early.description: Yellow slime on stem and leaves before heading.

mid.description: Twisting and distortion of central leaves and earhead.

late.description: Earheads fail to emerge or are completely covered in yellow bacterial mass.

solution.pesticide: Nematode control (disease is nematode-bacterial complex)
solution.application: Float seeds in 20% salt solution and skim off galls.
solution.organic_alternative: Summer ploughing to kill nematode cysts.

agro_zones: East Coast Plains and Hills Region

early.description: Nymphs and adults feed at the base of the plant.

mid.description: Leaves turn yellow then brown; honeydew promotes sooty mold.

late.description: 'Hopperburn' occurs; large circular patches of dead plants.

solution.pesticide: Pymetrozine 50% WG @ 120g/acre or Dinotefuran 20% SG @ 80g/acre
solution.application: Direct spray at the basal portion of the plant.
solution.organic_alternative: Alternate wetting and drying of the field.

early.description: Rosette flowers; larvae feed on squares and flowers.

mid.description: Larvae enter bolls, feed on seeds, preventing lint development.

late.description: Bolls fail to open fully, lint is stained and rotted.

solution.pesticide: Spinosad 45% SC @ 75ml/acre or Emamectin benzoate 5% SG @ 100g/acre
solution.application: Spray when 10% bolls/flowers are infested.
solution.organic_alternative: Pheromone traps (5/acre) and Trichogramma egg parasitoids.

agro_zones: East Coast Plains and Hills Region

early.description: Papery windows on leaves caused by early instar larvae.

mid.description: Extensive leaf defoliation with ragged edges and lots of frass.

late.description: Larvae bore into whorls and cobs, severely reducing yield.

solution.pesticide: Spinetoram 11.7% SC @ 100ml/acre or Chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC @ 80ml/acre
solution.application: Direct spray into the whorl of the plant.
solution.organic_alternative: Application of sand+lime into whorls or Metarhizium anisopliae.

early.description: Colonies on underside of young leaves sucking sap.

mid.description: Leaves curl downwards and become yellowish.

late.description: Heavy honeydew secretion leading to sooty mold, stunting.

solution.pesticide: Imidacloprid 17.8% SL @ 40ml/acre or Flonicamid 50% WG @ 60g/acre
solution.application: Spray when damage reaches Economic Threshold Level (ETL).
solution.organic_alternative: Neem oil spray (1500 ppm) @ 5ml/L.

early.description: Scraping of leaf epidermis, leaves show silvery patches.

mid.description: Leaves curl upwards, becoming brittle ('boat shape').

late.description: Severe flower drop, stunting, deformed fruits.

solution.pesticide: Fipronil 5% SC @ 2ml/L or Spinosad 45% SC @ 0.3ml/L
solution.application: Spray primarily focusing on the terminal growth.
solution.organic_alternative: Blue sticky traps (10/acre) and Neem oil.

mid.description: Moderate damage visible on plant structures.

solution.pesticide: Broad-spectrum control agent or consult local agricultural officer.
solution.application: Apply as per generic guidelines for this type of issue.
solution.organic_alternative: Neem oil spray (1500 ppm) @ 5ml/L or Trichoderma soil application.

solution.pesticide: Broad-spectrum control agent or consult local agricultural officer.
solution.application: Apply as per generic guidelines for this type of issue.
